name: Ranger
tagline: The professional zealot you learn the breach with.
overview:
Start here. The Ranger is the Pyre's baseline Purger, the balanced operator every player should understand first: medium armor, rifle up, average everything and reliable in all of it.

Not a clean sci-fi marine. A professional zealot in scorched gunmetal who does the job and walks back out of the breach.
visualMotifs: Furnace visor with blood-hot and hellfire orange heat lines

name: Bulwark
tagline: A walking furnace that stands in the breach longer than anyone should.
overview:
You are the slowest thing on the field and the last thing still standing. Bulwark is the Pyre's heavy Purger, slabbed in scorched plate and built to soak breach pressure that would erase anyone else. No Warden wall-builder here, just a furnace with legs and a cannon.

Plant your boots, vent heat through your chest, and let the Scourge break itself against you while your shotgun does the closing argument.
gameplayRead: Slowest but most durable Pyre avatar
visualMotifs: Broad scorched plates and reinforced boots
Furnace vents in chest, shoulders, and forearms
Blood-hot red heat scars under blackened armor
Cracked bone-ceramic panels from repeated breach descents

name: Vector
tagline: Stays alive by never standing still.
overview:
A breach runner with a death wish and a sensor suite. Vector is the Pyre's fast, light scout, sent in to mark breaches and kite swarms while everyone slower tries to keep up.

Slim armor over a black pressure suit, long running legs, and ember accents kept thin and quick. Speed is the build, and the build is staying alive.
gameplayRead: Sensor fins, grapnel, or breach-marker gear
visualMotifs: Light scorched armor over a black pressure suit

name: Patch
tagline: Cauterizes damage faster than the breach can finish you.
overview:
Forget clean doctors and white coats. Patch is a Pyre burn technician, sealing wounds shut with heat and keeping Purgers moving when the breach is doing its best to stop them.

Medium-light armor, an injector rig, and zero intention of standing in the back. Support that still pulls a trigger.
gameplayRead: Injector tubes, heat-seal tools, and support rig
visualMotifs: Bone-white shoulder plates with abstract marks
Blood-stained utility pouches and field repairs

name: Pyre Duelist
tagline: Pyre doctrine sharpened into arena pressure.
overview:
Sanctioned violence, where the Pact gets tested but never broken. The Duelist is the Pyre's arena-tuned fighter: lighter, faster, and built to close the distance before you can think about it.

Ember blade in hand, triangular and aggressive, this is a Purger who treats every match as a reason to get closer.
gameplayRead: Ember blade, carbide, or close-range heat weapon
visualMotifs: Scorched armor and ash cloth accents

name: Pyre Cauterizer
tagline: Every problem is just something to burn closed.
overview:
A portable breach furnace dropped into the arena. The Cauterizer is the Pyre's area controller, masked and pressure-sealed, dragging heat tanks and a short-range burner that turns whole zones into no-go ground.

Where Patch heals, you erase. Set the floor on fire and watch the duel become a survival problem.
gameplayRead: Heat tanks and short-range burner weapon
visualMotifs: Blood-hot red and hellfire orange heat scars

name: Pyre Interceptor Pilot
tagline: Burns the breach out of orbit before it ever falls into the lanes.
overview:
A Purger pointed at the sky. The Interceptor Pilot takes the Pyre's doctrine to cockpit scale, hunting Scourge breach matter in orbit so it never reaches the ground below.

Fast, fragile, and built to gamble. Offensive burn weapons and a risky upgrade tree for pilots who'd rather win hard than play safe.
gameplayRead: Offensive burn weapons and a risky upgrade tree
visualMotifs: Blood-hot warning lamps and hellfire weapon cues

name: Field Engineer
tagline: Turns broken ground into a defendable lane.
overview:
Somebody has to make the line hold, and that somebody is you. The Field Engineer is the Wardens' baseline builder, a practical technician working under fire with a tower-control rig and a harness full of clamps and repairs.

No glory, no heroics. Just put the towers up, keep them firing, and make sure the lane is still there when the Scourge arrives.
gameplayRead: Tower-control tablet, baton, or wrist rig
visualMotifs: Industrial yellow markings and bone stencils
Grease, scratches, weld scars, and field repairs

name: Lane Gunner
tagline: Stay planted and let the Scourge break itself on the line.
overview:
Attrition is a strategy, and you are the strategy. The Lane Gunner is the Wardens' heavy ranged defender: broad stance, fed ammo belt, and a weapon that does not stop until the lane is clear.

You win by not moving. Brace, feed, fire, and let the horde grind itself into the dirt in front of you.
gameplayRead: Reinforced knee braces and shoulder plates

name: Wallwright
tagline: Builds the last wall before a lane becomes a grave.
overview:
When the line is about to fall, you're what's left between it and the dirt. The Wallwright is the Wardens' shield-and-barricade specialist, a builder-protector hauling heavy field hardware into the worst of it.

Shield emitter on the back, barricade anchors on the arms, and a slab of chest armor between the Scourge and everyone behind you. No paladins here, just a wall that fights back.
gameplayRead: Deployable barricade anchors on arms or belt

name: Warden Artillerist
tagline: Brings siege math into sanctioned arena combat.
overview:
Aggression is for amateurs. The Artillerist is the Wardens' long-range arena hero, dropping controlled firepower from a braced stance while everyone else scrambles in the open.

Shoulder targeting rig, compact launcher, and a calm that comes from doing the math before the round even starts. You don't chase the fight. You range it.

name: Warden Bastion
tagline: Turns a duel into a fortified position.
overview:
The line holds, even inside a blood-arena. The Bastion is the Wardens' arena tank and support, deploying a shield projector to turn open ground into a position your team can actually live on.

Heavy but organized, square and deliberate. Where the Pyre heavy burns through, you simply refuse to give the ground back.
visualMotifs: Worn gunmetal slab armor with industrial yellow

name: Warden Defense Pilot
overview:
The lanes below stay safe because you hold the sky above them. The Defense Pilot is the Wardens' orbital protector, flying a sturdier profile built around shields, barriers, and keeping infrastructure intact.

No clean NASA fantasy here. Reinforced flight suit, shield-control harness, and a cockpit full of grime and war repairs. Defensive flying for pilots who'd rather everyone make it home.
gameplayRead: Sturdier ship and armor profile than the Pyre pilot
visualMotifs: Gunmetal plating and industrial yellow marks

name: Pyre Courier
tagline: Runs a burn order down the dead roads faster than the Choir can close them.
overview:
When the Scourge cuts the lanes and the wired relays go dark, the order still has to move — so you move it on foot, faster than the horde can re-route to cut you off.

The Pyre way: strip the armor, take the boost, and burn every repeater-pylon you pass. Stop, and the Choir's omniscience snaps shut around you. So don't stop.
gameplayRead: Light, forward-leaning silhouette built for pure momentum
Trades armor for top speed and aggressive boost
Clips repeater-pylons on the way past — each kill sends the local horde feral
visualMotifs: Stripped scorched plate and ash-cloth streamers
Blood-hot Pyre heat lines trailing the limbs
Furnace visor and a hellfire message canister

name: Warden Courier
tagline: Carries a hold order through the rot and intends to deliver it intact.
overview:
Someone has to get the hold order to the next holdout, and they intend to arrive with it intact. The Warden Courier trades the Pyre runner's reckless top speed for control and a cargo rig that survives contact.

Speed as a duty, not a gamble — keep momentum through the rot, protect the message like a wall, and don't let the lane between holdouts become a grave.
gameplayRead: Heavier, steadier runner than the Pyre Courier
Reinforced cargo rig — the order survives the hits
Holds momentum through contact instead of gambling on raw pace
visualMotifs: Gunmetal road armor with sparing industrial-yellow stripes
Armored, sealed message-case and courier harness
Ember rangefinder lamps, worn steel, weld scars

name: Pyre Saboteur
tagline: Climbs a living breach-ship to its heart and sets the core on fire.
overview:
A rothulk is a beached Scourge breach-ship grown into the ground, and you're the one Purger going inside it. Light, agile, and carrying a single ignition charge, you platform up the living nest toward the breach-core that keeps it connected.

This isn't a firefight — it's a climb. Stay out of the Choir's massed sight, reach the heart, set it on fire, and run as the severed node turns feral around you.
gameplayRead: Light, agile climber built for jumps, swings, and wall-work
Carries sabotage charges — reaching the core matters, not clearing the room
Survives by routing through the gaps in the Choir's massed sight
visualMotifs: Furnace visor dimmed to a sliver so the nest can't read the glow
A hellfire ignition charge at the hip
Bone-ceramic grips, blood-hot scars, rust and gore from the climb
